description: Audit infrastructure SLA/SLO definitions for UltrafastSecp256k1
pre_alert: pre_alert_buffer_days (Bastion B3): audit_sla_check.py emits a non-blocking PRE-ALERT warning while an artifact is within this many days of its blocking threshold, and reports days_until_block per artifact so the SLA never silently jumps from green to blocked.

max_stale_evidence_days.description: Maximum age in days for any critical evidence artifact before it is considered stale
max_stale_evidence_days.measurement: max(now - evidence_timestamp) across all critical evidence files

max_unresolved_high_finding_days.description: Maximum number of days a HIGH-severity finding may remain unresolved
max_unresolved_high_finding_days.measurement: max(now - finding_created) for findings with severity >= HIGH and status != resolved

ci_flake_budget_percent.description: Maximum percentage of CI runs that may flake (non-deterministic failure) over a 30-day window

critical_evidence_freshness_days.description: Maximum age in days for critical-path evidence (CT, determinism, fuzz, ABI negative)
critical_evidence_freshness_days.measurement: max(now - timestamp) for evidence tagged critical

gate_pass_rate_percent.description: Minimum percentage of preflight gate runs that pass over a 30-day window

exploit_to_regression_max_hours.description: Maximum hours between crash/exploit discovery and permanent regression test creation

determinism_golden_staleness_days.description: Maximum age of determinism golden reference before re-verification required

incident_drill_freshness_days.description: Maximum age of the incident-drill log (docs/INCIDENT_DRILL_LOG.json) before drills are considered stale. Advisory for now: drills must keep running, but until the nightly auto-commit loop is observed for a full window (cf. H-1) this is a warning, not a release block, to avoid a self-inflicted recurring block. Promote to blocking after the observation window.

integration_evidence_freshness.description: External integration evidence (libbitcoin / Bitcoin Core / libsecp shim) freshness, evaluated per-surface (Bastion B13). The per-surface freshness_days, severity (blocking | warning | owner_gated), and last_verified live in docs/INTEGRATION_EVIDENCE_STATUS.json and are enforced by ci/check_integration_evidence.py (audit_gate.py --integration-evidence, G-13). A blocking surface that is missing or stale fails; warning surfaces are advisory with a pre-alert; owner_gated full-chain validation is surfaced explicitly and never counted as current evidence.
integration_evidence_freshness.measurement: per-surface (now - last_verified) vs freshness_days; evidence_path existence for blocking rows

ct_evidence_status_freshness.description: Constant-time evidence freshness + tool-verdict binding, evaluated per CT-sensitive surface (Bastion B14). The per-surface evidence_paths, required_tools, freshness_days, severity, and last_verified live in docs/CT_EVIDENCE_STATUS.json and are enforced by ci/check_ct_evidence_status.py (audit_gate.py --ct-evidence-status, G-14). On push the committed-evidence + freshness dimension gates (a blocking CT surface fails if a committed path is missing or last_verified is stale). Tool verdicts (ct-verif / valgrind-ct / dudect) are evaluated only when a --verdict-dir is supplied (CI workflows): a required-tool FAIL or PASS+SKIP is inconclusive (never pass) and fails a blocking row. Distinct from the audit/ci-evidence 'ct_evidence' critical-evidence snapshot SLO.
ct_evidence_status_freshness.measurement: per-surface (now - last_verified) vs freshness_days; evidence_paths existence; required_tools PASS verdicts when --verdict-dir present

fuzz_campaign_freshness.description: Fuzz campaign evidence freshness + crash->regression conversion, evaluated per fuzz surface (Bastion B15). The per-surface corpus_path, crash_path, regression_path, freshness_days, severity, and last_verified live in docs/FUZZ_CAMPAIGN_STATUS.json and are enforced by ci/check_fuzz_campaign_status.py (audit_gate.py --fuzz-campaign-status, G-15). Evidence-status gate only (cheap; long campaigns run in cflite/cryptofuzz/klee, not on push): a blocking surface fails on missing corpus, stale/malformed last_verified, or a crash artifact without a matching regression (crash_unconverted); an unconverted crash blocks regardless of severity; owner_gated heavy/host-only surfaces are explicit and never current.
fuzz_campaign_freshness.measurement: per-surface (now - last_verified) vs freshness_days; corpus_path existence; crash_path artifacts vs regression_path presence

gpu_hardware_evidence_freshness.description: GPU / hardware evidence status freshness, evaluated per backend/surface (Bastion B16). The per-row backend, claim_type, evidence_path, hardware_required, freshness_days, severity, and last_verified live in docs/GPU_HARDWARE_EVIDENCE_STATUS.json and are enforced by ci/check_gpu_hardware_evidence.py (audit_gate.py --gpu-hardware-evidence, G-16). Cheap on push (no GPU hardware required): a blocking row fails on missing/stale evidence; owner_gated real-device rows are explicit and never current; documented_residual rows must resolve to a RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md id; a fallback_correctness row must name an existing fallback_path and is never native-performance evidence (a performance claim naming a fallback_path fails).
gpu_hardware_evidence_freshness.measurement: per-row (now - last_verified) vs freshness_days; evidence_path existence; claim_type/fallback_path integrity; residual_risk_id resolution

review_queue_max_open_days.description: Maximum age in days that an item may sit in the source-graph review queue without being addressed or explicitly deferred
review_queue_max_open_days.measurement: now - first_seen for each row in source_graph review_queue table without an explicit deferral marker

todo_max_open_days.description: Maximum age in days for a TODO/FIXME comment in source code without an explicit deferral marker
todo_max_open_days.measurement: git blame age of the line introducing the TODO/FIXME marker

freshness_artifacts.description: Bastion B19 (RR-BAS-01 / RR-BAS-02): explicit refresh-disposition ledger binding every freshness artifact tracked by ci/audit_sla_check.py to either an AUTOMATIC producer (a regeneration+commit step in a scheduled workflow) or a DOCUMENTED RESIDUAL (an authored spec, a stable golden, a build-only artifact, or an owner-chosen manual chore). Enforced by ci/check_evidence_refresh_coverage.py (audit_gate.py --evidence-refresh-coverage, G-19): a blocking freshness artifact with neither a verifiable auto-producer nor a residual that resolves to docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md fails the gate. `auto` entries are cross-checked against the named workflow's actual commit list so the manifest cannot claim coverage the workflow does not provide. Authored/golden artifacts are intentionally NOT nightly-regenerated (that would fake freshness on a human-authored or baseline artifact); their honest_refresh_mechanism documents what keeps them current instead.

refresh.producer_step: Refresh security autonomy KPI snapshot — ci/security_autonomy_check.py runs ci/incident_drills.py (weight 10), which rewrites docs/INCIDENT_DRILL_LOG.json
refresh.promotion: RR-BAS-02: incident_drill_freshness_days.severity is `warning` until the nightly auto-commit loop is observed green for one full window; promote to `blocking` per the RR-BAS-02 close condition (the ci/check_evidence_refresh_coverage.py self-test already proves audit_sla_check.py blocks a simulated stale log under blocking severity).

refresh.reason: CT / adversarial / fuzz output snapshots captured by building and running the four standalone audit binaries (test_adversarial_protocol, test_ecies_regression, test_fuzz_parsers, test_fuzz_address_bip32_ffi). The owner explicitly chose a manual chore + B3 pre-alert over an auto-committing scheduled lane (a CI-cost / human-review decision); auto-committing captured fuzz output unattended is declined by the owner.
refresh.honest_refresh_mechanism: Manual owner chore on a <14-day cadence; ci/audit_sla_check.py B3 pre-alert warns ~4 days before the block with a per-artifact days_until_block.

refresh.reason: Authored API-security-contract specification (api/criticality/ct_class/preconditions/postconditions/failure_modes/evidence_tests/evidence_docs), validated by ci/check_api_contracts.py. Regenerating it nightly would fake freshness on a human-authored spec.
refresh.honest_refresh_mechanism: ci/check_api_contracts.py fail-closed changed-file policy: sensitive API/security source cannot change without a docs/API_SECURITY_CONTRACTS.json update, so the contract is re-attested whenever the surface it describes changes.

refresh.reason: Authored assurance-claims ledger (schema_version/updated_at/source/claims), validated by ci/validate_assurance.py. Not a derived report — auto-regeneration would fake freshness.
refresh.honest_refresh_mechanism: Re-attested by the owner when claims change; the validate_assurance.py companion check binds each claim to its evidence on every push.

refresh.reason: Golden reference fixture. By definition it is intentionally stable — regenerating it nightly would defeat its purpose as a fixed baseline.
refresh.honest_refresh_mechanism: Re-verified (compared, not regenerated) by the determinism gate on every push; the staleness SLO is a re-verification prompt, not a regeneration trigger.

refresh.reason: Build-only artifact under the gitignored out/ tree; produced by the risk-surface gate during a build, never committed to the repo.
refresh.honest_refresh_mechanism: Regenerated on every CI build that runs the risk-surface gate; in a clean/standalone checkout its absence is downgraded to a warning by audit_sla_check.py (scope=build).

release_policy.description: Release readiness requires zero active blocking SLO violations
release_policy.rule: any blocking SLO violation -> release_ready = false
